event_name: AAA National Conference & Industry Expo (Australian Airports Association)

description: The peak annual gathering for Australia's airport sector, run by the Australian Airports Association, featuring a four-day program with a large industry expo, technical forums and the National Airport Industry Awards.
notes: The peak annual gathering for Australia's airport sector - four-day program with large industry expo, Women in Airports Forum, technical forums and the National Airport Industry Awards. Buyers span airport operators, ground services, security and infrastructure. 2026 dates left blank pending official announcement - do not infer from 2025 pattern.

description: The premier annual event for Australia's franchised new car and truck dealers, run by the AADA, featuring keynote speakers, workshops and an exhibition on dealership profitability, digital retail and industry trends.
notes: Premier event for franchised new car and truck dealers. 2026 theme 'Leading the Way' - 50+ speakers, 16 workshops covering dealership profitability, AI, digital retail. AADA represents ~1,500 dealers operating 3,000+ dealerships.

next_edition_expected: Mid-2027 (expected; 2026 edition was 17 Jun at MCEC, Melbourne)
venue: Rotates; 2026 edition at Melbourne Convention and Exhibition Centre
description: The Australian Banking Association's showcase annual conference, bringing bank CEOs and executives together with regulators, academics and thought leaders. Sponsorship and networking access rather than a trade floor. Useful for fintechs building credibility and relationships with the country's major and regional banks.
target_audience: Bank executives, regulators, policymakers and banking-sector suppliers
notes: 2026 edition (Banking Boldly, 17 Jun, MCEC) has passed; 2027 details not yet announced.

event_name: ACA Qld National Conference (Australian Childcare Alliance)

notes: National conference for early learning service owners/operators run by ACA Qld (theme 2026: 'Dreaming Big & Driving Change'), with sponsor and exhibitor program. 2026 edition has run (26-28 Jun, BCEC). Strong owner/operator buyer audience, though organised by the Queensland branch.

event_name: ACCA Annual Conference & Trade Exhibition (Cemeteries & Crematoria)
description: ACCA's annual conference and trade exhibition - the 2026 edition marks the association's 40th anniversary with keynotes, workshops and a dedicated trade exhibition.

notes: Officially the 37th Annual Conference & Trade Exhibition; 2026 also marks ACCA's 40th anniversary as an association. Program highlights to be announced; exhibitor and sponsor EOIs open via [email redacted].

description: A large free-to-attend trade exhibition and conference for the accounting, finance and business software world, with around 150 exhibitors and 3,700 attendees. Fintech, payments, lending and practice-software vendors exhibit to a professional buying audience of accountants and finance decision-makers. A proven, low-cost stand format for international entrants testing the Australian market.
target_audience: Accountants, bookkeepers, CFOs, finance leaders, payroll professionals and business owners
notes: 2026 edition (18-19 Mar, ICC Sydney) has passed; 2027 dates confirmed on the official FAQ page.

description: An annual one-day conference and expo for accounting professionals, run by Momentum Media's Accountants Daily, covering innovation, business strategy and regulation.
notes: Third annual ACE conference & expo for accounting professionals - innovation, business strategy and regulation. 2026 edition has run (Fri 12 June, Hyatt Regency Sydney, principal partner Savvywise); 2027 dates to be announced. One-day format, smaller than the Accounting & Business Expo (already tracked separately).

description: A one-day dental industry CPD symposium and boutique supplier exhibition in Adelaide, run by the Australian Dental Industry Association, offering two learning streams and keynote speakers.
notes: One-day CPD symposium with boutique supplier exhibition (~41 exhibitors), two learning streams and keynote speakers. Smaller than ADX but a useful SA touchpoint for dental industry vendors. Frequency not formally stated by ADIA.

organiser: ADMA (Association for Data-driven Marketing and Advertising)

target_audience: Data-driven marketing practitioners, CMOs and martech decision makers
notes: 2026 date (8 September) confirmed by ADMA's CEO via the association's official channels; an aggregator lists Sofitel Sydney Wentworth as venue - unverified officially, so venue left null.

description: Queensland's premier dental trade event, run by the Australian Dental Industry Association, offering a free trade exhibition, CPD program and Learning Labs; sister event to ADX Sydney.
notes: Queensland's premier dental trade event, launched by ADIA in 2022. 70+ exhibitors across 10,000m²+ at BCEC; free to attend with CPD program, Learning Labs and complimentary childcare. Sister event to ADX Sydney (biennial, next March 2028). Exhibition hours Thu-Sat.

next_edition_expected: March 2028 (expected; biennial - ADX26 was 26-28 Mar 2026 at ICC Sydney)
description: Australia's premier dental event, held biennially at ICC Sydney with over 13,500 m² of exhibition space showcasing products and technology from across the dental industry. Suppliers exhibit directly to dentists and practice owners attending for the trade floor and CPD programme. The flagship route to market for dental equipment and technology companies entering Australia.
target_audience: Dentists, dental practice owners, dental technicians and dental industry buyers
notes: ADX26 (26-28 Mar 2026) has passed; next Sydney edition expected March 2028 (date not yet published). Companion events: ADX Brisbane 8 Oct 2026 (BCEC) and Adelaide Dental Symposium 21 Aug 2026 (Adelaide CC, 41 exhibitors).

description: The Australian Furniture Association's industry forum on the future of the Australian furniture industry, including a Circular Economy Activator session, VIP dinner and networking events, focused on moving the sector from interest in circularity to informed action.
target_audience: Furniture designers, manufacturers, suppliers, retailers and industry partners
notes: 2026 edition has run (24 February 2026, RMIT Melbourne). Ticketing tiers vary by AFA membership type. Frequency of the series not formally published.

organiser: AFAC & Deutsche Messe (Hannover Fairs Pty Ltd)
description: Australasia's largest emergency management conference and exhibition, run by AFAC with Deutsche Messe, featuring a free-to-attend expo, 100+ speakers and concurrent streams including the Australian Disaster Resilience Conference; rotates jurisdictions annually.
notes: Australasia's largest emergency management conference and exhibition - free-to-attend expo runs 18-20 Aug 2026, conference through 21 Aug (100+ speakers; concurrent streams incl. Australian Disaster Resilience Conference, WAFA and IFE National Conference). Rotates jurisdictions annually (2025 Perth, 2026 Melbourne). Named Best Trade Show and Best Event 2024 at the Australian Business Event Awards. Buyers: fire agencies, SES, government emergency management.

next_edition_expected: March 2027 (expected; 2026 edition was 16 Mar at Hilton Sydney)

Access is via delegate tickets and sponsorship rather than an exhibition floor. High-value visibility and relationship-building for fintechs selling into the big four and regional banks.
target_audience: Bank CEOs and senior executives, investors, regulators and financial services advisers
notes: 2026 edition has passed; AFR Live already has a Banking Summit 2027 page live without dates. APRA's event listing corroborates the 16 Mar 2026 edition.

description: The leading annual event for Australia's aged care, retirement living and seniors housing sectors, run by the industry's peak body. A multi-day trade exhibition runs alongside the plenary and concurrent programme, putting care technology, equipment and service vendors directly in front of provider organisations. A strong buying audience for healthtech and assistive technology companies targeting the aged care market.
target_audience: Aged care, retirement living and seniors housing providers, executives and procurement decision-makers
notes: Trade exhibition confirmed in the published programme (bump-in 23 Nov, bump-out 26 Nov). Ageing Australia also runs state conferences (e.g. WA 3-5 Jun 2026, NSW/ACT 12-14 Aug 2026).

description: The peak industry body's flagship Melbourne trade fair, where hundreds of gift and homewares brands present to retail buyers ahead of the Christmas trading cycle. For consumer product vendors, it is one of the two core sourcing windows in the Australian retail calendar.
target_audience: Gift, homewares and lifestyle retailers, wholesalers, corporate and hospitality buyers
notes: Runs 8-12 August 2026 at MCEC, concurrently with the competing Reed Gift Fairs Melbourne. AGHA also runs the Sydney Gift Fair and launched the boutique 'Gifted: Melbourne' showcase (31 Jan - 2 Feb 2026).

description: AGHA's Sydney sourcing fair, repositioned to September 2026 to sit ahead of the Christmas trading period and better match retail buying cycles. Three days of boutique and emerging-brand sourcing at Sydney Showground for retailers across NSW.
target_audience: Gift and homewares retailers, boutique buyers, stylists, designers and event planners
notes: AGHA cancelled its February 2026 Sydney fair and consolidated into the 19-21 September 2026 edition, citing the clash of two concurrent Sydney fairs (per Australian Giftguide, Oct 2025).

description: Australia's flagship grain industry conference, hosted by Grain Trade Australia. 2026 theme: 'Thriving Today, Resilient Tomorrow'. Includes welcome drinks and gala dinner networking.
target_audience: Grain market participants, traders, exporters, logistics and service providers across the grain supply chain
notes: Official site and Grain Trade Australia both confirm 29-30 July 2026, Crown Promenade Melbourne.

venue: AgQuip Field Days site, 134 Black Jack Road

notes: Exhibitor applications for 2026 open via the official site; returning exhibitors get first option on sites.

description: Asia Pacific's premier agrifood innovation event, bringing the global agtech ecosystem - producers, startups, scaleups, investors and policymakers - to Adelaide for two days. The natural landing pad for agtech vendors entering the Australian market and for ANZ agrifood founders raising their profile.
target_audience: Agrifood tech startups, producers, researchers, investors and corporate agribusiness
notes: 2027 edition confirmed for 16-17 February at Adelaide Convention Centre (Adelaide previously hosted in 2023); Welcome Event 15 February at the National Wine Centre.

event_name: AHICE Asia Pacific - Hotel Industry Conference & Exhibition

notes: 2027 dates not yet announced - dates left null. Part of an 11-event global AHICE series including a Sydney Hotel Summit (31 July 2026, Hilton Sydney) and AHICE Aotearoa.

Strong channel for proptech/SaaS vendors targeting agencies.

notes: Sydney (13-14 May) and Brisbane 2026 editions have passed. Next: Bendigo Local Edition 20 Oct 2026, then Hobart 4 Mar 2027, Perth 19-20 May 2027 and Melbourne 26-27 May 2027. Mixed B2B/consumer audience - triaged medium.

description: A public-sector summit on data, AI and data governance for Australian government agencies, run by Akolade with a tiered sponsor and exhibitor program.
notes: Public sector data, AI and data-governance summit with sponsor/exhibitor program (platinum through exhibitor tiers). Official site currently promotes the 2027 edition.

notes: 2026 edition ran 25-26 March with record 4,200+ visitors. 12th edition in 2027.

event_name: Australasian Pharmacy Professional Conference & Trade Exhibition (APP)

notes: 2026 edition ran 9-12 June at MCEC; 2027 dates (21-24 June) confirmed on the official site. Quest Events also ran fintech/banking events captured in Run 001.

description: The biggest healthcare exhibition in the southern hemisphere, running for over 15 years and drawing 10,000+ attendees across the full health ecosystem. The free-to-attend expo puts vendors in front of hospital, government and aged care decision-makers, with dedicated zones spanning digital health, diagnostics, infrastructure and workforce. A proven, high-volume floor for both international entrants and scaling locals.
target_audience: Hospital executives, clinicians, digital health leaders, aged care providers, government and procurement teams
ticket_price_range_aud: 0 (free expo pass); paid conference passes available
notes: 2026 edition (11-12 Mar) has passed; 2027 confirmed for 17-18 Mar at ICC Sydney, co-located with the Healthcare 2040 Expo. New for 2027: Investor Zone and Future of Diagnostics Zone. Attendance figure is the organiser's published claim.

organiser: IDP Education and the International Education Association of Australia (IEAA)
description: The premier forum for Australia's international education sector, celebrating its 40th anniversary in Sydney with four days of programme, exhibition and networking. The key event for edtech, student-services and recruitment vendors targeting institutional buyers.
target_audience: University and education-provider leaders, government, agents and international education service providers
notes: Attendance figure (1,600+ delegates) from the event's official channels. Early bird registration closes 18 September 2026.

description: Australia's largest furniture trade show - a three-day, trade-only buying and networking event connecting retail chains, online retailers, independents and commercial buyers with 200+ furniture exhibitors. 2026 theme: Alchemy.
target_audience: Furniture retail chains, online retailers, independent furniture retailers, commercial/project buyers and interior designers
notes: Co-located with Decor + Design (already tracked separately in this database) at MCEC, 15-17 July 2026.

description: The Australian Investment Council's flagship annual conference and the largest gathering for private capital professionals in Australia, covering private equity, venture capital and private credit trends over two days of keynotes, panels and networking, including the Investing for Growth Awards & Gala Dinner.
target_audience: Private equity and venture capital fund managers (GPs), institutional investors (LPs), advisers, corporate partners and government
notes: 2026 edition returns to the Gold Coast; all plenary sessions at The Langham with opening night drinks at Surfers Pavilion. Dress code business casual.

next_edition_expected: 10-11 April 2027, Cairns
description: Trade-only fair where Australia's jewellery industry kicks off the year - fine jewellery, gemstones and design from leading suppliers. Sister event to the International Jewellery Fair.
target_audience: Jewellery retailers and designers sourcing at the start of the year
notes: Ran 19-20 Apr 2026 in Adelaide. 2027 edition moves to Cairns, 10-11 April (per organiser's official Instagram).

next_edition_expected: 2027, Melbourne (expected; official site states 'We hope to see you in Melbourne in 2027, details coming in late 2026' - 2026 edition ran 12-14 May at Brisbane Convention & Exhibition Centre)
description: Australia's premier precision and advanced manufacturing exhibition, run for over 25 years by peak body AMTIL and rotating between major cities. The core route to market for machine tools, automation and manufacturing technology vendors.
target_audience: Precision and advanced manufacturers - production engineers, plant managers and capital equipment procurement
notes: 2026 Brisbane edition (first time in Queensland) has just passed; 2027 Melbourne dates not yet announced.

venue: ANFD site, Borenore (15km west of Orange)
description: Australia's oldest annual agricultural exhibition, established in 1952, drawing hundreds of exhibitors to the Borenore site near Orange each October. A heritage fixture for vendors targeting mixed farming and regional agribusiness buyers.
target_audience: Farmers, producers and rural businesses across central-west NSW and beyond
notes: Regional listings cite 500-600 exhibitors; figure not published on the official site so left out of the exhibitor_count field.

description: The peak national convention for regional tourism in Australia, hosted in a different regional destination each year. A sponsorship and networking channel for vendors selling into regional tourism bodies and councils.
target_audience: Regional tourism organisations, operators, local government and destination-marketing decision makers
notes: 2026 host destination (Busselton, WA) announced by ART October 2025; program is welcome reception + two convention days + dinner. Attendance figures not published - left null.

description: A long-running, numbered series of forums for Australia's space sector, run by the Andy Thomas Space Foundation, combining conference sessions with a dedicated Exhibition Hall showcasing the global space ecosystem.
notes: 19th Australian Space Forum - 21-22 Jul 2026, theme 'Global Challenges, Regional Solutions: Space Partnerships in the Indo-Pacific'. Includes a dedicated Exhibition Hall (21 Jul) showcasing the global space ecosystem, plus networking function and media stage. Recurring series (numbered editions, roughly annual/twice-yearly cadence) - frequency left blank.

description: Australia's leading space industry summit and exhibition, run by Momentum Media's Space Connect, bringing together government, industry and Defence speakers alongside an exhibition floor.
notes: Australia's leading space industry summit & exhibition, fifth year in 2026 (has run 17-18 Jun). 70+ speakers across government, industry and Defence; principal partner Space Centre Australia. 2027 dates not yet announced.

organiser: Tourism Australia (ATE27 in partnership with Destination NSW)
description: Australia's largest annual travel and tourism B2B event, connecting Australian tourism sellers with international buyers through scheduled business appointments. The Southern Hemisphere's biggest international tourism trade conference, returning to Sydney in 2027 for the first time in five years.
target_audience: Australian visitor-economy businesses and international tourism wholesalers, retailers and buyers
notes: ATE26 ran 10-14 May 2026 at Adelaide Convention Centre with 2,900+ attendees and buyers from 32+ countries (official ministerial media release). Appointment-based marketplace format rather than open exhibition.

venue: Art Gallery of New South Wales
description: A one-day summit from the Australian Investment Council connecting investors, founders and ideas, with deep dives into venture capital trends, emerging industries and portfolio company case studies through panels and presentations.
target_audience: Venture capital investors, founders, entrepreneurs, advisers and corporate innovation leaders
notes: 2026 edition has run (28 May 2026, Sydney). Corporate partners include Hall & Wilcox and HLB Mann Judd.

description: The Australian Veterinary Association's flagship annual conference, featuring a multi-stream scientific program, trade exhibition and a Start-Up Hub highlighting innovation in the veterinary sector.
notes: AVA's flagship annual conference - 2026 edition has run (18-21 May, Brisbane; first time back since 2018). 8-stream scientific program, trade exhibition, Start-Up Hub highlighting vet-sector innovation. 2027 dates/host city not yet announced. AVA also runs smaller state conferences (e.g. Tasmanian Conference, 13-15 Nov 2026, Launceston).

event_name: Avalon - Australian International Airshow & Aerospace/Defence Exposition
description: The Southern Hemisphere's most prestigious aviation, aerospace and defence event, combining three dedicated industry trade days with a massive public airshow. The 2025 edition generated $240.9 million for the Victorian economy, underscoring its scale as a market-entry platform.
target_audience: Aerospace and defence primes, MRO, advanced air mobility, government and RAAF procurement (trade days), plus public audiences
notes: Trade days 23-26 February; public days 26-28 February. Attendance figure (200,000+ visitors, 2025) from the official dates announcement and includes public days. A US pavilion organiser cites 902 exhibitors - third-party, noted only.

event_name: AVCJ Private Equity Forum Australia & New Zealand
next_edition_expected: March 2027, Sydney (dates TBC)
description: The region's leading LP-GP gathering for private equity, now in its 23rd year. The 2026 forum brought together over 750 private market professionals including 290+ LPs representing 410+ organisations from 21 countries, held in Sydney and online.
target_audience: Private equity decision-makers: LPs (super funds, sovereign funds, endowments), GPs, fund-of-funds, advisers and service providers
notes: 2026 edition has run (3-5 March, Sydney + online). Hybrid format. Keynotes in 2026 included Hon Peter Costello AC and Pacific Equity Partners' Tim Sims AM.

description: A focused two-day conference for B2B ecommerce at manufacturers and distributors, part of a global series with editions in the US and Europe. It is a niche but well-matched audience for vendors selling B2B commerce platforms, PIM and digital sales tooling into the ANZ market.
target_audience: Digital and ecommerce leaders at manufacturers and distributors across Asia Pacific
notes: Asia Pacific edition confirmed for 28-29 July 2026 in Melbourne on the official site; specific venue not yet published.

description: APAC's largest conference for B2B marketing leaders, celebrating its 12th year in 2027 with 70+ speakers, four tracks and the B2B Marketing Hall of Fame Awards dinner. Directly relevant to MES's own audience of B2B vendors building ANZ pipeline.
target_audience: B2B CMOs and marketing leaders across technology, professional services and industrial sectors
notes: 2026 edition ran 20-21 May at the same venue (~90 speakers per aggregator). Sister event: B2B Marketing Leaders Forum ASIA, 14-15 October 2026, Singapore.

description: Australia's largest professional, trade-only beauty event, run by RX Australia, bringing together salon owners and beauty buyers with brand exhibitors, conference sessions and the Face2Face Makeup Awards.
notes: Australia's largest professional beauty event - trade only. 7,000+ key decision makers, motivated buyers and salon owners; 200+ brands expected. Includes 60+ conference sessions and the Face2Face Makeup Awards.

description: Australia's national beef expo and one of the world's leading beef cattle events, running for a full week once every three years in Rockhampton. Its scale and international delegations make it a marquee market-entry moment for vendors across genetics, agtech, processing and export services.
target_audience: Beef producers, processors, exporters, scientists and agribusiness suppliers
notes: Triennial cadence - outside the prompt's frequency enum (annual/biennial/multiple_per_year); flagged for the validation script. Next edition 2-8 May 2027.

next_edition_expected: March 2027 (expected; 2026 edition was 25 Mar at ICC Sydney)
description: The annual summit of Australian Payments Plus, the operator of Australia's domestic payments and digital identity infrastructure (eftpos, BPAY, NPP, ConnectID). The 2026 edition drew more than 700 leaders from banking, fintech, government and technology. A strong room for payments and identity fintechs to meet scheme operators and bank partners.
target_audience: Leaders across banking, fintech, government and technology in payments and digital identity
notes: 2026 edition has passed; 2027 date not yet announced. Attendance figure is AP+'s own published wrap-up claim.

description: The Southern Hemisphere's biggest music industry gathering - a daytime industry conference plus ~100-artist showcase festival across Fortitude Valley, attracting 1,700+ delegates and 16,000+ aggregate showcase attendance.
target_audience: Music industry executives, labels, publishers, booking agents, media, brands and artists
notes: Official site confirms 1-4 September 2026. Conflicting dates on musick.com.au (8-10 Sep) disregarded - official site wins. Attendance figure is conference delegates.

description: Australia's largest industry exhibition and the biggest truck show in the Southern Hemisphere, run by Heavy Vehicle Industry Australia, showcasing trucks, trailers, components, equipment and transport technology.
notes: Australia's largest industry exhibition and the biggest truck show in the Southern Hemisphere - trucks, trailers, components, equipment and transport technology. 2027 edition (20-23 May, BCEC) marks the event's 60th anniversary. Attendance/exhibitor figures from the record 2025 edition (54,790 attendees, 250+ exhibitors over 35,000m²). Includes Truck Week on South Bank and Heritage Truck Show.

next_edition_expected: 2027 (Melbourne - alternates Sydney/Melbourne annually)
description: Australia's largest trade event for the convenience and impulse retail industry, running for over 30 years. Showcases suppliers from across Asia Pacific with products, services and equipment for convenience retailers, headlined by the C&I Industry Symposium.
target_audience: Convenience, petrol & convenience (P&C), roadside retail, independent grocery, impulse and route trade retailers and buyers
ticket_price_range_aud: Free for retailers and trade buyers
notes: 2026 edition (35th) has run, 17-18 March at ICC Sydney. Alternates between Sydney and Melbourne each year, 130+ exhibitors.

description: The caravan and camping industry's biggest national business event - keynotes, breakout sessions, expanded trade exhibition, masterclasses and gala awards dinner.
target_audience: Caravan & RV manufacturers, dealers, holiday park operators and industry suppliers
notes: 2026 edition set a record: 1,150+ delegates and 140 exhibitors (AccomNews coverage). Runs back-to-back with the CPAQ state conference.

next_edition_expected: 22-24 October 2026, Nyaal Banyul Geelong Convention and Event Centre
venue: Nyaal Banyul Geelong Convention and Event Centre
description: The annual national conference of the Community Broadcasting Association of Australia, bringing the community radio and broadcasting sector together for professional development, awards and networking. The 2026 edition moves to the new Nyaal Banyul Geelong Convention and Event Centre.
target_audience: Community radio and TV station managers, broadcast staff and volunteers, sector suppliers and media technology vendors
notes: Registrations, award entries and scholarship applications for 2026 are open; exact 2026 dates were not visible in research sources - left blank rather than inferred.

description: CDAO Melbourne is Corinium's flagship Victorian gathering of 500+ senior data and AI leaders, co-located with the Data & AI Architecture and Enterprise AI events at Crown Promenade. Sponsorship puts data and analytics vendors directly in front of the executives who own enterprise data budgets across both public and private sectors.
target_audience: Chief Data & Analytics Officers and senior data, AI and governance leaders across finance, government, healthcare, retail and energy
notes: 500+ attendees and 70+ speakers published on official Corinium site. Co-located with Data & AI Architecture and Enterprise AI Melbourne.

next_edition_expected: March 2027 (expected; 2026 edition ran 3-4 March, co-located with Data & AI Architecture Sydney and Enterprise AI Sydney)
description: CDAO Sydney is Australia's longest-running senior data and analytics leadership event, convening C-suite data officers for keynotes, panels and case studies on data readiness, responsible AI and governance. Its co-located format with Data & AI Architecture and Enterprise AI Sydney gives vendors a concentrated two-day window with NSW's most senior data buyers.
target_audience: C-level and senior data, analytics and AI leaders across Australian enterprise and government
notes: 2026 edition (3-4 March, Sydney) has passed; official site does not yet show 2027 dates. Aggregators indicate 2-3 March 2027 - not confirmed on official site, so dates left null.

description: Australia's leading trade show for intralogistics, robotics and automation, warehousing and materials handling, part of the global CeMAT series. Free to attend, it is the key platform for warehouse automation vendors targeting Asia-Pacific buyers.
target_audience: Warehouse operators, supply chain managers and logistics leaders buying automation, robotics and materials handling
notes: 2026 edition ran 23-25 June at MCEC; 2027 confirmed for 27-29 July at The Dome, Sydney Showground. Alternates Sydney/Melbourne. Global series also runs in Singapore, Shanghai, Guangzhou, Milan and Istanbul.

organiser: ANZ Federation of Chemical Engineers (with IChemE, RACI & Engineers Australia)
description: Annual conference for the Australian & NZ chemical and process engineering community, co-located with the Hazards Australasia process safety conference. 2026 theme: Innovate. Integrate. Impact.
target_audience: Chemical & process engineers, industrial chemists, process safety professionals
ticket_price_range_aud: $1,010-$2,190 AUD (member/non-member/student-retiree tiers; early bird to 17 Jul)
notes: Single registration covers both Chemeca and Hazards Australasia. 2025 edition was in Adelaide. Early bird extended to 17 Jul 2026.

description: One of Victoria's most significant annual forums for senior information security leaders, gathering 500+ attendees and 50+ speakers at Crown Promenade. Co-location with Cloud Security, AppSec & DevSecOps and OT Security Melbourne concentrates several security buying audiences under one roof for sponsoring vendors.
target_audience: Victoria's senior information security leaders - CISOs, heads of cyber, GRC and resilience executives
notes: Attendance (500+) and speaker count from the official event site. Co-located with Cloud Security Melbourne, AppSec & DevSecOps Melbourne and OT Security Melbourne. 2026 edition runs 14-15 July - days away at time of research.

description: Corinium's flagship NSW security summit, billed as the largest annual gathering of the state's most senior InfoSec decision makers. Its sponsor-driven format with exhibition space and facilitated networking makes it an efficient way for vendors to reach concentrated CISO buying power in Sydney.
target_audience: NSW's most senior InfoSec decision makers - CISOs, heads of security, risk and resilience leaders
notes: 2027 dates (16-17 Feb) per Corinium's own events calendar. 2026 edition ran 10-11 February at Royal Randwick Racecourse; aggregators list Royal Randwick for 2027 but the venue is not yet confirmed on the official event site.

next_edition_expected: May 2027 (annual; running since 1996)
description: A long-running regional waste management conference and trade exhibition held in Coffs Harbour, drawing waste industry professionals from across the sector.
notes: Long-running waste management conference (est. 1996, 30-year legacy) with trade exhibition. 2025 edition drew 670+ delegates in person and online. 2026 edition has run. Organising company not clearly published - left blank.

next_edition_expected: May 2028 (biennial; co-located with Australian Auto Aftermarket Expo)
description: Australia's most comprehensive exhibition of crash repair equipment, tools and body finishing materials for the collision repair trade, run by the AAAA and co-located with the Australian Auto Aftermarket Expo.
notes: Nation's most comprehensive exhibition of body finishing materials, crash repair equipment and tools. Co-located with AAAE, forming Australia's largest combined automotive aftermarket gathering.

description: Australia's flagship critical communications conference and exhibition, bringing radio, LTE, satellite and public safety technology suppliers together with the government and enterprise buyers who specify and purchase that equipment. The free exhibition pass draws a broad procurement audience alongside the paid conference. The key route to market for critical comms and private network vendors in Australia.
target_audience: Those who select, purchase, install and maintain critical communications and public safety equipment across government, emergency services, utilities, transport and resources
ticket_price_range_aud: Free exhibition-only pass; paid one- and two-day conference packages
notes: Pre-conference technical workshops run 13 Oct 2026 with association partners ARCIA and ACCF. A New Zealand edition (Comms Connect Wellington, 27-28 May 2026, 400+ users and 30+ exhibitors) runs separately - excluded from this directory as it is not held in Australia.

next_edition_expected: June 2027 (expected; 25th edition was 2-3 Jun 2026 at the Fullerton Hotel, Sydney)
description: Australia's pre-eminent telecommunications industry conference, now past its 25th edition, gathering around 400 of the sector's most senior leaders at the Fullerton Hotel each year. Extensive sponsorship tiers (Platinum through Silver, plus awards sponsorships) give technology vendors direct access to the CEOs of Australia's network operators, retailers and regulators. The essential room for any international telecom vendor entering the Australian market.
target_audience: CEOs and senior executives of network operators and retailers, global technology vendors, regulators and government
notes: 2026 edition (2-3 Jun, with pre-Summit reception 1 Jun) has passed; 2027 date not yet published. Organiser also runs the CommsDay Wholesale Congress and has historically co-run the Australasia Satellite Forum.

description: Two days of conference, exhibition and deal-making across Australia's wholesale telecommunications ecosystem, designed as the meeting place between the companies that build and operate communications infrastructure and the service providers that rely on it. Explicitly buyer-seller in format, helping resellers, MVNOs and MSPs understand their wholesale options. A highly targeted floor for infrastructure and connectivity vendors entering the Australian market.
target_audience: Wholesale infrastructure providers (fixed access, dark fibre, backhaul, towers, data centres, international connectivity) and the resellers, MVNOs and MSPs who buy from them
notes: Returns to Sydney in 2026 after several Melbourne editions. Early bird discount of $500 per registration available at time of research; agenda published July 2026.

organiser: Connected Technology Alliance (formerly IoT Alliance Australia)
description: The annual summit of Australia's connected technology peak body, successor to the IoT Impact conference, bringing together the leaders, practitioners and policy makers responsible for connected systems. The three-day program combines plenaries, technical roundtables (including with the Department of Home Affairs), networking drinks and an anniversary awards dinner. A focused room for IoT, wireless and connectivity vendors building Australian channel and government relationships.
target_audience: Technology leaders, IoT and connectivity practitioners, and policy makers across enterprise, infrastructure and government
notes: Dates derived from the official program (Day 3 confirmed as Thursday 3 Sep 2026; three-day program implies 1-3 Sep). Venue/city not yet published on the pages reviewed - predecessor IoT Impact ran at ICC Sydney with 120 speakers and 50+ exhibitors. Post-rebrand exhibition scale unverified - triaged medium.

description: A two-day expo for the civil construction, infrastructure and commercial equipment sector at MCEC, returning in 2027 after a successful inaugural 2025 event. A route to contractor and council buyers for machinery and works-technology vendors.
target_audience: Civil construction, infrastructure and municipal works contractors, councils and equipment buyers
notes: Inaugural edition ran September 2025; 2027 dates (15-16 September) confirmed on the official site - biennial cadence inferred from the 2025→2027 pattern.

organiser: Communication and Public Relations Australia (CPRA, formerly PRIA)
description: The national conference of Communication and Public Relations Australia (formerly PRIA), bringing Australia's communicators together to tackle AI's impact on the profession, trust and responsible influence. 2026 theme: Reimagine, Redefine, Refocus.
target_audience: PR, communications and corporate affairs professionals, agency leaders and in-house comms teams
notes: Principal partner Influencing; national partner Isentia. Group discount of 10% for 3+ registrations.

event_name: CS4CA ANZ Summit (Cyber Security for Critical Assets)
description: The ANZ edition of the global Cyber Security for Critical Assets series, returning to Perth for its third year with 100+ senior IT/OT security leaders and a claimed 3:1 end-user ratio. Its WA base places vendors close to the resources and utilities operators driving much of Australia's OT security spend.
target_audience: Senior IT and OT cyber security leaders from Australian and NZ critical infrastructure - energy, water, transport, mining and airports
notes: 3rd edition confirmed for 9-10 February 2027 in Perth on the official site; 2026 edition ran 10-11 February in Perth. Venue for 2027 not yet published. Speakers drawn from Western Power, Water Corporation, Perth Airport and Sydney Airport.

description: Forefront's state-based cyber summit series, running Victorian, Queensland and Western Australian editions each year with a two-stream IT and OT security format. The intimate, senior-level delegate profile suits vendors seeking quality conversations with cyber decision makers rather than volume foot traffic.
target_audience: CISOs, CIOs, heads of cyber, risk and technology leaders across IT and OT security
notes: Next upcoming edition: Perth, 11 November 2026 at Crown Perth per Forefront's official Cyber Community channel; Forefront's 2026 schedule PDF earlier listed WA on 11 June, so the November date reflects the more recent organiser announcement. VIC 2026 (4 March) drew 250+ leaders; QLD 2026 ran 13 May at BCEC. Editions typically gather 130-250+ delegates.

description: Forefront Events runs its Data & AI Summit as a state-based series, with the next edition in Melbourne after the NSW edition ran in March 2026. The one-day, partner-driven format delivers a concentrated room of 250-300 data and AI decision-makers, making it a low-cost, repeatable touchpoint for vendors building an ANZ pipeline.
target_audience: Heads of data, analytics, AI/ML and data science across financial services, government and retail
notes: One row per series: next edition VIC 22 October 2026 at MCEC (official event page; organiser's schedule PDF shows 21 October - event page date used). NSW edition ran 31 March 2026 at ICC Sydney. Organiser publishes 250-300 delegates per event.

description: The inaugural Australian edition of Informa's global Data Center World series, co-located with The AI Summit Australia at MCEC. It brings the digital infrastructure ecosystem - operators, hyperscalers, telcos and the vendors that supply them - together around AI-ready infrastructure, power and connectivity. A major new exhibition floor for infrastructure vendors targeting Australia's data centre boom.
target_audience: Data centre operators and developers, hyperscalers, telcos, enterprise IT leaders and infrastructure investors
notes: First Australian edition, part of Data Center World's 2026 global expansion into Asia, Australia, Brazil and Europe (backed by the established annual US flagship and the AFCOM community). Qualifies via confirmed scheduled date; watch for recurrence confirmation after the first edition.

description: Australia's dedicated conference and exhibition for the leaders building, powering and investing in AI infrastructure, covering market dynamics, capital flows, power, cooling and site selection. Partner packages offer direct access to operators, hyperscalers and investors through exhibition, roundtable hosting and lead-generation formats. A senior, deal-oriented room for infrastructure and energy vendors entering the Australian data centre market.
target_audience: Data centre operators and developers, hyperscalers and AI companies, enterprises deploying AI, energy providers and infrastructure investors
notes: 4th annual edition; formerly the Data Centre Real Estate Summit. 2026 edition (17-18 Mar, Sydney Event Centre at The Star) has passed; 2027 confirmed for the Hilton Sydney.

description: Data Innovation Summit ANZ is the Australian edition of Hyperight's global applied data and AI event series, running 30+ speakers across three stages with networking in a dedicated exhibition area. Its practitioner-led, case-study format suits data platform and analytics vendors seeking hands-on buyers rather than purely executive audiences.
target_audience: Data, analytics and AI practitioners and leaders applying data innovation across ANZ organisations
notes: Date (17 September 2026, Sydney) confirmed on official Data Innovation Summit global site; venue not yet published.

description: Australia's premier interiors trade event, run by Informa, showcasing furniture, lighting, textiles, art and surfaces to interior designers, architects and retailers; co-located with the Green Design Show.
notes: Australia's premier interiors trade event - 10,000+ design professionals expected (interior designers, architects, retailers, specifiers); 200+ exhibitors across furniture, lighting, textiles, art and surfaces. Co-located with the Green Design Show. Free trade visitor registration.

description: The flagship event for Australia's architecture & design community, showcasing products, materials and ideas to architects, interior designers, specifiers and developers as part of Design & Build Week.
notes: Flagship event for the architecture & design (A&D) community - products, materials and ideas for architects, interior designers, specifiers and developers. 2026 edition ran 11-13 June at ICC Sydney as part of Design & Build Week (with Futurebuild Australia and Kitchen+Bath Show); 2027 edition moves to MCEC Melbourne. Partners include Design Institute of Australia and RAIA.

description: Australia's premier mining investment forum, in its 35th year at Kalgoorlie, combining presentations from ~66 listed miners and explorers with a large exhibition area for mining services and suppliers. Its concentration of decision makers and dealmakers is unmatched in the sector.
target_audience: Mining and exploration companies, brokers, bankers, investors, financiers and mining services
notes: Third-party references cite 2,500+ delegates and a ~$2,250 three-day pass (2023) - not official, so left out of the structured fields. Runs 3 weeks after this research date.

next_edition_expected: June 2027 (expected; 2026 edition was 25 Jun at ICC Sydney)
description: Forefront Events' annual digital banking summit, run across dedicated customer experience and banking technology streams at ICC Sydney. Vendors engage through sponsorship and partner slots alongside senior bank decision-makers. A focused setting for fintechs selling digital banking, CX and core technology into Australian banks.
target_audience: Senior banking leaders across customer experience and banking technology
notes: 2026 edition has passed. A third-party listing cites 300+ delegates - unverified against the official site, so attendance left null.

description: A one-day summit bringing senior banking and finance leaders together at ICC Sydney to cover digital transformation, fintech innovation and AI in financial services. Sponsorship and partner access rather than a full expo floor. Useful for vendors selling into NSW-based banks and financial institutions.
target_audience: Senior banking and financial services leaders across digital, technology and customer experience
notes: Clashes with Intersekt (Melbourne) in the same week of Sep 2026.

description: Australia's leading digital health event, drawing 8,000+ health leaders across two days with 400 speakers, 11 theatres and 300+ exhibitors. Health tech vendors exhibit directly to hospitals, government, primary care and aged care buyers in a fast-paced festival format. The single best floor in the country for a healthtech company entering or scaling in the Australian market.
target_audience: Health system executives, clinicians, digital health leaders, government and aged care decision-makers
notes: DHF26 (20-21 May 2026, MCEC) has passed; DHF27 confirmed for 19-20 May 2027. Attendance and exhibitor figures are the organiser's published claims.

description: Australia's premier early childhood education conference, run by Early Childhood Australia, bringing together service leaders, educators and policy makers over four days with a trade exhibition.
notes: Australia's premier early childhood education conference - four days with trade exhibition. 2026 theme: 'Holding the Past, Creating the Future'. Audience: service leaders/directors, educators, policy makers and sector partners - key buyers of edtech, resources and services for early learning.

description: Australia's largest education event - the biggest education festival in the Asia-Pacific - pairing a free-to-attend exhibition with 10+ parallel paid conferences. The definitive platform for edtech vendors selling into Australian schools, universities and VET providers.
target_audience: K-12, higher education and vocational education leaders, teachers and edtech procurement decision makers
notes: 2026 edition ran 3-4 June at ICC Sydney with 340+ speakers and 240 EdTech providers (official event app); 2027 dates (9-10 June) confirmed on the official Terrapinn site - 2027 venue not yet stated, so venue left null. Third parties cite 10,000 attendees / 320+ exhibitors - noted only.

next_edition_expected: June 2027 (expected; 2026 edition ran 2-4 June at Kingsthorpe Park, Toowoomba; long-established annual event)
description: Queensland's largest annual commercial agricultural business event, showcasing farm machinery, irrigation, agtech and agribusiness services on the Darling Downs. A key state-level route to market for rural vendors.
target_audience: Queensland farmers, producers and agribusiness buyers across the Darling Downs
notes: 2026 edition (2-4 June) has passed; 2027 dates not yet announced. Naming-rights sponsor: Elders.

event_name: Energy Exchange Australia (EXA, formerly AOG Energy)
next_edition_expected: March 2027 (expected; 2026 edition ran 10-12 March at Perth Convention & Exhibition Centre; held annually in Perth for over 40 years)
description: Australia's flagship upstream energy supply-chain exhibition, rebranded from AOG Energy after 40+ years, pairing a large exhibition floor with a free conference programme. Its Perth base puts vendors in front of the operators and EPCs driving Australia's resources and energy-transition capital projects.
target_audience: Upstream energy operators, engineers, suppliers and technology providers across oil, gas, decommissioning, wind, CCS and hydrogen
notes: 2026 edition (10-12 March) drew 221 exhibitors per the official recap; 2027 dates not yet announced. WA Government promotes the event as the state's premier energy supply-chain gathering.

description: The Australian edition of WBR's global eTail series, now in its fifth year, combining conference content with curated one-on-one meetings between retailers and technology partners. Its interactive format favours vendors seeking structured, pre-matched conversations with ecommerce decision makers.
target_audience: Senior ecommerce and digital retail executives from Australian retailers and brands
notes: Dates (22-23 September 2026) confirmed by organiser listings. Venue conflict: aggregators list Pullman Melbourne Albert Park while earlier editions ran in Sydney; venue left blank pending confirmation on the official site.

description: An EV and home-energy consumer/trade expo from the Fully Charged Show (Everything Electric) brand, showcasing electric vehicles, charging and home energy technology to Sydney audiences.
notes: EV & home-energy expo. 2026 Sydney edition postponed from March to 18-20 September citing 'global EV headwinds'; Melbourne edition not returning in 2026 (2027 under consideration). Hybrid B2B/B2C format; Sydney is the brand's second-largest global audience city after London.

description: The flagship national congress for Australia's financial advice profession, run by the Financial Advice Association Australia, featuring three days of sessions, a trade exhibition and the FAAA Awards.
notes: Flagship national congress for Australia's financial advice profession - three days of sessions, exhibition and the FAAA Awards. Key event for fintech/advice-tech vendors selling to advisers and licensees.

description: The 50th International Fundraising Conference - Australasia's largest gathering of professional fundraisers, with an exhibitor/sponsor marketplace. 2027 theme: Imagine What's Possible.
target_audience: Fundraisers, charity leaders, fundraising technology & services suppliers
notes: 2027 dates/location per FIA's official LinkedIn (24-26 Feb 2027, Gold Coast); venue not yet announced. 2026 edition was 25-27 Feb at MCEC Melbourne (Impact Amplified).

description: The Southern Hemisphere's largest trade-only event for foodservice, hospitality and retail, with 900+ exhibitors from 30+ countries across 12 product zones. The definitive route to market for food and beverage brands entering Australia, alternating annually between Melbourne and Sydney.
target_audience: Foodservice, hospitality and retail buyers - chefs, restaurateurs, caterers, distributors and grocery
notes: Exhibitor figure (900+) from the official visit page. The 2025 Sydney edition drew 26,054 visitors and 853 exhibitors per the US pavilion organiser - third-party, noted only.

next_edition_expected: Late 2026 (13th annual edition announced for Sydney; date not yet published)
description: A long-running annual summit themed around the four pillars of fintech and banking: challenger banking, payments, digital assets and banking technology. It brings together the technology companies redefining Australian financial services with bank and fintech decision-makers. Worth tracking for sponsorship once the 2026 programme and date are published.
target_audience: Fintech and banking technology leaders, challenger banks, payments and digital asset firms
notes: 13th annual edition confirmed for Sydney but programme, date and venue not yet announced. Organiser details not clearly published on the event site - capture on next verification pass.

description: The APA's flagship conference, running for more than a decade. FOCUS26 (Elevating practices, empowering physiotherapy) targets the business and leadership side of physiotherapy practice.
target_audience: Physiotherapists, practice owners, clinic leaders
notes: Abstract submissions opened 29 Jan 2026.

next_edition_expected: May 2028 (biennial pattern; 2026 edition ran 12-14 May)
venue: Gold Coast Convention and Exhibition Centre
organiser: Poultry Information Exchange (PIX), Australian Pork Limited & Australasian Milling Conference
description: Australia's largest combined poultry, pork and feed-milling industry event - PIX, Australian Pork Limited and the Australasian Milling Conference under one roof, with 200+ exhibition booths (largest yet in 2026).
target_audience: Poultry and pork producers, stockfeed/milling manufacturers, equipment, genetics, animal health and feed ingredient suppliers
notes: Has run for 2026. The only Australian conference dedicated to compound feed manufacturers (AMC). Pan Pacific Pork Expo is folded into this combined event.

description: Australia's leading trade event for food processing, packaging and manufacturing innovation, covering everything from processing equipment and ingredients to plant technology. The key buying window for food manufacturing capital equipment vendors.
target_audience: Food and beverage manufacturers - production, engineering, packaging and procurement leads
notes: Runs 26-29 July 2026 - two weeks after this research date. Triennial cadence - outside the frequency enum; flagged for validation.

description: A national professional gathering for Australia's forestry sector, run by Forestry Australia, alternating between a full Conference in odd years and a Symposium in even years, with a policy/professional focus and modest supplier presence.
notes: National professional gathering for the forestry sector - alternates between full Conference (odd years, e.g. Adelaide 2025) and Symposium (even years). 2026 theme: 'The forestry story: strategies for improved awareness, social licence and effective engagement'. Policy/professional focus with modest supplier presence - medium vendor-buyer relevance.

event_name: Franchising Expo (+ Start Your Own Business Expo)
next_edition_expected: Melbourne 1-2 Aug 2026 is the next stop; 2026 tour: Brisbane 21-22 Mar, Sydney (ICC) 30-31 May, Melbourne 1-2 Aug
description: A touring expo for prospective and existing franchisees and small business owners, run by Specialised Events, featuring exhibitors, free seminars and expert advice; runs alongside the co-ticketed Start Your Own Business Expo.
notes: Touring franchise and business-opportunity expo series with 100+ exhibitors, free seminars and expert advice; runs alongside the Start Your Own Business Expo (one ticket covers both). Row dated to next upcoming stop (Melbourne); venue for Melbourne edition not confirmed on official site.

venue: Novotel Sunshine Coast Resort, Twin Waters
description: The biggest event in Australia's timber frame and truss manufacturing / offsite prefabrication industry, run by FTMA Australia, combining conference sessions with a dedicated trade expo day.
notes: The biggest event in Australia's timber frame & truss manufacturing / offsite timber prefabrication industry. 2026 edition (theme 'At the Crossroads: Reframing for Growth') has run 18-20 May and was reported as record-breaking. Trade expo day 19 May. Next edition dates not yet announced - frequency not formally stated (recent editions roughly annual/biennial), left blank.

description: National convention of Australia's peak funeral industry body - professional development, industry discussions and supplier sponsorship/networking opportunities.
target_audience: Funeral directors, funeral home operators and industry suppliers
notes: Ran 19-23 Mar 2026. National and divisional sponsorship packages offered.

event_name: Future of Financial Services: Data and AI
description: FST Media's forum for data-driven finance, gathering 200+ senior leaders and technology partners to shape AI and data strategy across Australian financial services. Vendors participate as sponsors and technology partners rather than expo exhibitors. A targeted room for data, AI and infrastructure fintechs selling into banks, insurers and super funds.
target_audience: Data, AI and technology executives at Australian banks, insurers and superannuation funds
notes: 2nd annual edition. FST Media runs a wider 'Future of' series plus the Banking Innovation Awards (10 Sep 2026).

description: Australia's leading exhibition for building innovation and sustainable construction, uniting manufacturers and solution providers with architects and builders as the industry works to deliver 1.2 million new homes by 2030. A focused platform for green building and construction-innovation vendors.
target_audience: Architects, builders, specifiers and decision makers focused on building innovation and sustainability
notes: 2026 edition ran 11-13 June at ICC Sydney as part of Design & Build Week (alongside Kitchen+Bath Show and Design Show Australia); 2027 dates (22-23 September, ICC Sydney) confirmed on the official site.

description: Australia's leading professional games industry conference, run by IGEA, bringing together game developers, publishers and investors; kicks off Melbourne International Games Week and closes with the Australian Game Developer Awards.
notes: Australia's leading professional games industry conference, celebrating 20 years in 2026. Kicks off Melbourne International Games Week; closes with the Australian Game Developer Awards (AGDAs) on 7 October. Specific venue not yet published.

event_name: Gartner Cybersecurity & Risk Management Summit Australia
description: Gartner's flagship APAC security and risk event, bringing CISOs and senior risk leaders to ICC Sydney alongside a dedicated exhibitor showcase of security solution providers. Exhibiting puts vendors directly in front of budget-holding security executives, while ANZ scale-ups can use it to track enterprise buying priorities.
target_audience: CISOs, security and risk management leaders, heads of cloud and data security across APAC enterprises
notes: Renamed from 'Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit'. 2026 edition ran 16-17 March at ICC Sydney; 2027 edition confirmed for 15-16 March 2027 on the official site.

venue: Gold Coast Convention and Exhibition Centre
description: Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo is Australia's most senior enterprise technology gathering, combining the analyst-led conference with a dedicated Xpo exhibition floor at the Gold Coast Convention and Exhibition Centre. For international software vendors it is the single best room of Australian CIO budget-holders; for ANZ founders the Xpo offers direct exposure to enterprise and government buyers evaluating their next technology investments.
target_audience: CIOs and senior IT executives from Australia's largest enterprises and government agencies
notes: Dates confirmed on official Gartner site. Attendance figures only available via aggregators - left null per verification rules.

description: Australia's trade-only sourcing exhibition connecting international manufacturers with Australian retail and brand buyers, running twice yearly in Sydney and Melbourne. For offshore manufacturers, it is the most direct vendor-to-buyer channel into the Australian consumer products market.
target_audience: Retailers, brands and wholesalers sourcing apparel, textiles, homewares and consumer products from global manufacturers
notes: Sydney 2026 edition (16-18 June, ICC Sydney, 600+ exhibitors per official news) has passed; next edition 17-19 November 2026 at MCEC, confirmed officially. Exhibitor figure from the official site's Sydney announcement.

description: Government Innovation Week Federal bundles Public Sector Network's Digital Leadership Day, Government Innovation Showcase and Government Cyber Security Showcase into two days in Canberra. The showcase format is sponsor-driven, giving technology vendors structured access to federal digital and data leaders three months after Tech in Gov - useful as a second Canberra touchpoint in the same buying cycle.
target_audience: Federal government senior executives and digital, data and cyber specialists
notes: Second edition (inaugural ran 11-12 November 2025). Dates confirmed via PSN LinkedIn announcement and Intermedium listing; runs alongside sub-events at National Convention Centre Canberra.

description: Australia's leading conference and exhibition series for primary care, run in Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane and Perth each year. Healthcare companies exhibit the latest medical innovations and practice solutions to GPs and primary healthcare professionals attending for accredited CPD education. A repeatable, multi-city route to the general practice buyer base for medtech and health software vendors.
target_audience: GPs, practice nurses, practice managers, registrars and allied health professionals in primary care
notes: Next edition: Melbourne 31 Jul-2 Aug 2026 (MCEC). Also in 2026: Brisbane 12-13 Sep and Perth 19-20 Sep; Sydney returns 21-23 May 2027 at ICC Sydney (2026 Sydney edition passed).

next_edition_expected: Regional series runs each Feb-Mar; 2026 flagship Perth Update ran 16-17 Feb 2026 at Optus Stadium (~750 attendees); 2027 NSW one-day updates announced for Corowa (11 Feb), Condobolin (18 Feb) and Warialda (4 Mar)
description: GRDC's national program of grains research update events - flagship two-day updates in Perth and Adelaide plus dozens of regional one-day updates across grain-growing regions each year.
target_audience: Grain growers, agronomists, advisers, researchers and agtech/input suppliers
notes: Touring/regional series - dates left blank per one-row-per-series rule; specific editions listed in next_edition_expected. Attendance figure is for the Perth flagship update.

organiser: AUSVEG and the International Fresh Produce Association
description: The Southern Hemisphere's premier horticulture conference and trade show, converging the entire fresh produce industry for four days of trade exhibition, awards and networking. Vendors in agtech, packaging, logistics and farm inputs get direct access to grower and retailer buyers.
target_audience: Vegetable, fruit and floral growers, packers, retailers and horticulture supply chain buyers
notes: Australia & NZ's premier horticulture conference and trade show - growers, packers and supply chain decision-makers. 2026 edition ran 1-4 Jun at Adelaide Convention Centre; Hort Connections 2027 officially announced (Jun 2026) for 7-10 Jun 2027 at MCEC, Melbourne. Note: the hortconnections.com.au About page stale-lists 'Adelaide Convention Centre' for the 2027 dates - the organiser's official June 2026 announcement (Facebook/Instagram) confirms Melbourne; conflict noted.

event_name: HR + L&D Innovation & Tech Fest Australia
organiser: Hannover Fairs Australia (Tech Fest series, Deutsche Messe)
description: Australia's leading HR and L&D technology festival, uniting people leaders with the HR-tech vendor community around the future of work. A strong showcase channel for HR software entering the Australian market.
target_audience: HR and L&D leaders, people-technology buyers and workforce transformation teams
notes: Sister events: HR + L&D Tech Fest NZ (Auckland) and Legal Innovation & Tech Fest - same organiser as CeMAT Australia (Run 012).

description: The IDC CIO Summit Australia is the analyst firm's flagship one-day gathering of Australian technology decision-makers, with 2026 themed around agentic systems and AI-native enterprises. IDC's sponsorship model is built around vendor-to-CIO engagement, with a majority of delegates at C-level or executive leadership across large enterprise and the public sector.
target_audience: CIOs and senior technology decision-makers from large enterprise and public sector
notes: Date (20 October 2026) confirmed via IDC Asia/Pacific sponsorship calendar; venue (Sofitel Sydney Darling Harbour, Magnifique Ballroom) on official event site.

description: The region's premier commercial maritime and naval defence exposition, strongly supported by the Royal Australian Navy and the Department of Defence, with an international exhibition and the co-located International Maritime Conference. Sydney has been its home since 2000.
target_audience: Naval and merchant marine decision makers, defence industry, shore services and government from around the world
notes: 2025 edition ran 4-6 November at ICC Sydney; 2027 dates (2-4 November) confirmed on the official site and AMDA's events page.

next_edition_expected: February 2027, Sydney (expected; annual - InsurtechLIVE26 ran 18 February 2026 at Doltone House, Jones Bay Wharf, and the official site flags InsurtechLIVE27)
description: Australia's premier insurtech conference, connecting carriers, startups, investors and technology providers with keynotes, a startup pitch competition and networking. The natural landing pad for international insurtechs entering the Australian market.
target_audience: Insurers, insurtech startups, investors and technology providers across APAC
notes: One-day format; 2027 dates not yet announced - dates left null per verification rules.

description: ANZ's premier AV and integrated-technology trade event, run by Diversified Australia, showcasing suppliers across technology zones and co-located with the Security Exhibition & Conference and Fire Safety Expo.
notes: ANZ's premier AV & integrated technology trade event, 17th year. 120+ suppliers across 10 technology zones. Co-located with Security Exhibition & Conference and Fire Safety Expo - combined 2025 figures were 12,800+ attendees and 250+ exhibitors across the co-located shows.

description: The largest annual dairy event in the Southern Hemisphere - a week-long expo, trade show, seminars and one of the world's top five dairy cattle shows, held every January in the Goulburn Valley.
target_audience: Dairy farmers, cattle breeders, genetics companies, equipment and service suppliers
notes: 2026 edition ran 17-22 Jan with ~4,500 attendees from 20+ countries. Strong trade show component but centred on the cattle show - relevance rated medium for tech vendors.

description: Australia's premier trade-only jewellery and timepiece event, uniting domestic and international exhibitors under one roof at ICC Sydney Darling Harbour.
target_audience: Jewellery & timepiece retailers, designers, wholesalers and buying groups
notes: Attendance (~6,600) and exhibitor (~250) figures are third-party estimates (10times/exhibitions listings), not from the organiser. Free entry for registered trade.

event_name: International Mining and Resources Conference + Expo (IMARC)
description: Australia's largest mining event, uniting global mining leaders with technology, finance and government across six concurrent conferences and a 500+ exhibitor expo. The co-located Mines and Money @ IMARC investment forum adds capital-markets buyers, making it the sector's premier vendor platform.
target_audience: Mining executives, investors, government, METS suppliers and technology buyers across the full mining value chain
notes: Founding partners: Victorian Government, Austmine, AusIMM and Mines and Money. 2027 edition already confirmed for 12-14 October at ICC Sydney. Exhibitor figure (500+ solution providers) from the event's official channels.

description: Australia's flagship fintech festival, hosted by FinTech Australia as the centrepiece of Fintech Week Australia. It gathers the country's fintechs, banks, regulators, policymakers and investors for two days of talks, roundtables, satellite events and heavy networking. The single best room in the country for an international fintech scoping the Australian market or a local founder scaling up.
target_audience: Fintechs, banks, regulators, policymakers, investors and corporate innovation buyers
notes: Official site and FinTech Australia both list 3-4 Sep 2026; Fintech News Australia lists 2-3 Sep - official dates used. 10th edition.

next_edition_expected: 13-16 September 2027, Crown Perth
organiser: Institute of Public Works Engineering Australasia (IPWEA)
description: The biennial flagship conference of the Institute of Public Works Engineering Australasia, the key gathering for public works and infrastructure asset management professionals across Australia and New Zealand, with an exhibition of civil engineering and public works technology suppliers.
target_audience: Public works engineers, council and government infrastructure/asset managers, civil engineering suppliers and consultants
notes: Held every two years; the 2024 edition was at MCEC Melbourne. IPWC27 will be held at Crown Perth - 2027 dates not yet published. IPWEA also runs state conferences and an annual Fleet Conference.

description: New national irrigation trade show launched by Expertise Events for 2026, bringing together agriculture, urban greening and water-management sectors around precision and sustainable irrigation technology.
target_audience: Irrigation professionals across agriculture, urban greening and water management; equipment and precision-irrigation suppliers
notes: Inaugural edition - one of four new Expertise Events launches for 2026 (announced via Australasian Leisure Management). Frequency assumed annual per organiser's calendar positioning; monitor. A sister 'Irrigation Precinct' also features at Greenscapes Show Brisbane 5-6 Aug 2026.

description: Oceania's largest B2B trade event for the cleaning, hygiene and facility solutions industry, run by ISSA Oceania & Asia Pacific, connecting procurement teams, facility managers and distributors with suppliers; alternates annually between Melbourne and Sydney.
notes: Oceania's largest B2B trade event for the cleaning, hygiene and facility solutions industry - procurement teams, facility managers, distributors and contractors. Alternates annually between Melbourne and Sydney; 2026 edition at MCEC 8-9 Oct. Note: ISSA's global shows page stale-lists 'ICC Sydney' for the same dates - official event site (MCEC Melbourne) wins. Replaced the former AUSCLEAN Pulire and CleanScene shows.

next_edition_expected: 2027 (annual)
description: Australia's leading aged care innovation and technology conference, hosted by Ageing Australia, featuring keynotes, panels and a trade exhibition focused on digital transformation in aged and community care.
notes: Australia's leading aged care innovation and technology conference, hosted by Ageing Australia. Two days of keynotes, panels and a trade exhibition focused on digital transformation in aged and community care. 2026 edition has run.

next_edition_expected: 2028 (expected; biennial - 2026 edition ran 7-10 July at Adelaide Convention Centre with 2,000+ delegates and 100+ trade exhibitors)
description: The world's premier sheep industry event, uniting 2,000+ delegates from Australia, New Zealand, North America and the UK with a 100+ exhibitor trade hall. A concentrated route to livestock producers and processors for animal health, agtech and equipment vendors.
target_audience: Sheep, lamb and wool producers, processors, advisors and supply chain leaders
notes: 2026 edition (7-10 July, Adelaide) concluded the day before this research run; next edition expected 2028. Attendance/exhibitor figures from MLA and Livestock SA event listings; price from MLA.

event_name: Land Forces - International Land Defence Exposition
description: Australia's largest land defence industry exhibition, held biennially with the support of the Australian Army and rotating between major cities. The seventh edition in Perth is the key interface for defence vendors from primes down to SMEs seeking Army and Department of Defence buyers.
target_audience: Defence Force customers, prime contractors, SMEs and international land defence delegations
notes: Runs alongside the Chief of Army Symposium 2026 at the same venue. Complements the MilCIS row (Run 005) which covers military ICT specifically.

description: A one-day forum for L&D professionals run by Connect Media, positioned as a premier event for the sector with a focus on actionable insights, real-world solutions and partner/vendor engagement opportunities.
target_audience: L&D leaders, heads of capability, learning technology buyers and HR executives
notes: 13 August 2026 at RACV, Melbourne. Sponsorship-driven format with tailored partner packages.

organiser: Hannover Fairs Australia (Tech Fest series, Deutsche Messe)
description: A two-day immersion in the technologies and ideas shaping the legal industry - the only Australian event uniting law firm leaders, in-house counsel and legaltech vendors at scale. 2027 themes centre on generative and agentic AI in legal practice.
target_audience: Law firm business and technology leaders, senior corporate counsel and legaltech providers
notes: 2026 edition ran 28-29 April at the same venue; attendees can claim up to 5 CPD points. Same organiser as HR + L&D Tech Fest and CeMAT Australia.

description: The pre-eminent policy-making event of the NSW local government year, bringing together councillors and council staff from across the state alongside a partner exhibition program, run by Local Government NSW.
notes: The annual policy-making event for all NSW councils - the pre-eminent event of the NSW local government year, with a partner exhibition program (partnership prospectus published). Dates/venue confirmed via LGNSW official channels.

description: Australia's premier geospatial conference, running alongside the 10th International Digital Earth Summit in 2026 - 1,000+ expected attendees and ~50 industry exhibitors.
target_audience: Geospatial, surveying and location intelligence professionals across government, infrastructure, agriculture, energy, mining and emergency response
notes: Locate acquired by Geospatial Media & Events (Intermedia) after the former Geospatial Council of Australia wound up. Attendee/exhibitor estimates from Surveyors Australia media release and Spatial Source.

description: Australia's leading integrated supply chain and logistics conference and expo, bringing the end-to-end freight and logistics industry under one roof at MCEC. The 2026 exhibitor list includes Amazon, Maersk, DP World, UPS and WiseTech Global - evidence of top-tier commercial pull.
target_audience: Freight, warehousing, ports, infrastructure and distribution operators and decision makers
notes: Biennial cadence per multiple listings; registration runs through organiser Prime Creative Media's platform. Co-located with the Australian Bulk Handling Expo.

description: Victoria's largest construction and design show, with 500+ speakers across 17 conference stages plus Government and Architect's Hubs. Co-located with InstallerSHOW Melbourne, The Security Event Australia and The Fire Safety Event Australia in 2026, widening the buyer pool across the built environment.
target_audience: Victorian developers, architects, engineers, project managers, contractors and government
notes: Official site cites both '15,000+' and '20,000+' attendees and 200-300+ exhibitors in different places - conflicting, so structured figures left null. Sister event to Sydney Build.

description: A 10-day umbrella program connecting Australia's games sector with industry, education and public audiences across Melbourne CBD venues, run by ACMI and encompassing GCAP, education symposiums and the consumer festival PAX Aus.
notes: 10-day umbrella program across Melbourne CBD venues connecting Australia's games sector with industry, education and public audiences. Running since 2015, now managed by ACMI. Includes GCAP (industry), education symposiums, investment forums, showcases; PAX Aus (consumer festival, 9-11 Oct at MCEC) runs within the week.

organiser: MilCIS (event managed by Consec - Conference and Event Management)
description: Australia's only conference focused strategically on military communications and information systems, spanning the cyber and space domains with both a conference and a vendor expo. For suppliers of secure communications, networking and cyber capabilities, it is the annual touchpoint with Defence and defence-industry buyers in Canberra.
target_audience: Department of Defence personnel, defence contractors, government ICT buyers and researchers in the cyber and space domains
notes: Hybrid format - MilCIS Conference plus MilCIS Expo, in-person and online. Exhibitor and sponsorship programme via the official site; registration opens early 2026 with early-bird pricing to 28 August 2026. Major exhibitors include L3Harris and BAE Systems.

description: The premier medical technology industry conference in Australia, run by the sector's peak body and bringing together leaders from industry, government and academia. The two-day programme includes a trade exhibition where medtech vendors showcase innovations, plus the MedTech Industry Awards. The key relationship-building event for medtech companies selling into the Australian health system.
target_audience: Medical technology executives, hospital and health system leaders, regulators, government and academia
notes: Rescheduled from 15-16 Oct 2026 to 2-3 Mar 2027, remaining at ICC Sydney. MTAA also runs MedTech on the Hill (Canberra advocacy programme), the RegConnect Summit and the Women's Health MedTech Summit.

next_edition_expected: March 2027 (Crown Sydney, TBC)
description: Australia's premier PR and communications conference, run by Mumbrella at Crown Sydney, covering reputation, influence, measurement and AI in communications, culminating in the CommsCon Awards gala the same evening.
target_audience: Communications leaders, senior brand marketers, PR agencies and corporate affairs professionals
notes: 2026 edition has run (25 March 2026). Includes the CommsCon Awards recognising PR excellence across Australia and NZ.

next_edition_expected: May 2027, Sydney (expected; annual - the 2026 edition, its 15th, ran 26-28 May at Carriageworks with 100+ speakers across 10 content streams)
description: Australia's most important media and marketing industry conference, bringing together senior marketers and agency leaders for three days of content and networking. The flagship sponsorship platform for martech and media vendors targeting Australian CMOs.
target_audience: Senior marketers, media agencies, publishers and adtech/martech vendors
notes: 2027 dates not yet announced - dates left null per verification rules.

next_edition_expected: Mid-2026, Brisbane and Melbourne editions (expected; annual dual-city format - 2025 editions ran in both cities; dates via official site)
description: An annual dual-city (Brisbane and Melbourne) education event combining paid PD conferences with a free education expo and seminars. A cost-effective exhibition route for education vendors targeting school-level buyers.
target_audience: K-12 principals, school leaders and teachers seeking professional development and classroom solutions
notes: 2026 dates were not published in a verifiable form at research time - dates left null per verification rules. New Early Learning and Early Career Educator streams announced for 2026.

venue: Hotel Grand Chancellor Convention and Exhibition Centre
organiser: Family Law Section, Law Council of Australia
description: The 21st National Family Law Conference - the flagship biennial gathering of Australia's largest legal practice area, with a full exhibition and sponsorship programme. Registrations for 2026 sold out well in advance, underlining its commercial pull.
target_audience: Family law practitioners, judiciary and legal services providers nationwide
notes: Registrations exhausted with a waitlist operating - exhibitor/sponsor access via the Family Law Section. Theme: "Landscapes of Thought".

event_name: National General Assembly of Local Government (NGA)
next_edition_expected: June 2027 (annual; held in Canberra since 1994)
description: The largest annual gathering of Australian local government leaders - mayors, shire presidents and councillors - run by the Australian Local Government Association as part of Local Government Week.
notes: Largest annual gathering of local government leaders in Australia - regularly attracts 1,000+ delegates (mayors, shire presidents, councillors). 32nd NGA; part of Local Government Week 2026. 2026 edition has run.

event_name: NFDA Conference (National Funeral Directors Association of Australia)
organiser: National Funeral Directors Association of Australia (NFDA)
description: Three days of networking, learning, fellowship and connection for the NFDA community from across Australia.
target_audience: Funeral directors and funeral service professionals
notes: Source is the organiser's own Eventbrite listing; no standalone conference website found.
