description: The insurance broking profession's flagship national event, combining plenaries, workshops and a dynamic sponsor marketplace. The 2026 edition runs 30+ sessions with 80+ speakers under the theme "Future Ready".
target_audience: Insurance brokers at all career stages, insurers, underwriting agencies and service providers
notes: 2025 edition ran at the same venue (19-21 October) - Gold Coast is its established home.

description: Australia's largest annual hotel and accommodation industry event, covering the whole hospitality supply chain from property technology to design and guest experience. The key vendor-to-buyer floor for suppliers targeting accommodation operators.
target_audience: Owners, operators and decision makers from hotels, motels, serviced apartments, resorts, pubs, clubs and bars
notes: Attendance (6,000+) and exhibitor (200+) figures from the official Terrapinn site. Terrapinn also runs a NoVacancy London spin-off.

description: The Optical Distributors and Manufacturers Association's optical industry trade fair, held in even years alternating with the larger ODMAFair, covering lenses, frames, sunglasses and equipment; free for trade visitors.
notes: Optical industry trade fair - lenses, frames, sunglasses and equipment. Free for trade visitors. O-SHOW runs in even years, alternating with the larger ODMAFair (odd years). O-SHOW 26: Sun 16 - Mon 17 Aug 2026 at MCEC.

description: Australia's flagship optical industry trade fair, run biennially by the Optical Distributors and Manufacturers Association, covering lenses, frames, contact lenses, equipment and practice services; free for visitors.
notes: Australia's flagship optical industry trade fair - lenses, frames, contact lenses, equipment, practice services. Free for visitors. Biennial (odd years), alternating with O-SHOW. ODMAFair25 ran 27-29 June 2025 at ICC Sydney with record attendance; ODMAFair27 confirmed for Fri 13 - Sat 14 Aug 2027 at MCEC (per ODMA announcement via mivision, Apr 2026).

description: Australia's largest ecommerce conference and expo, running for over 15 years and drawing thousands of retail professionals to ICC Sydney each July. With 150+ exhibiting solution providers and 200+ speakers, it is the primary shop window for retail tech vendors entering the Australian market and a fast way for ANZ brands to benchmark their online operations.
target_audience: Retailers, ecommerce managers, digital marketers, brand leaders and retail technology buyers
notes: Organised by RX (Reed Exhibitions). Incorporates the Online Retail Industry Awards (ORIAS). Exhibitor count (150+) from official visit page; official site cites 'thousands' of attendees without a specific figure.

next_edition_expected: 32nd National Conference & Exhibition, 9-11 Jun 2027, Melbourne Convention & Exhibition Centre
description: Australia's largest clinical conference for occupational therapists. 2026 theme: From Ideas to Impact, with streams spanning older persons, disability, paediatrics, assistive technology and knowledge translation.
target_audience: Occupational therapists across disability, paediatrics, aged care & assistive technology
notes: Ran 23-24 Jun 2026. OTA alternates OT Exchange with its full National Conference & Exhibition - OTAUS 2027 is planned for Melbourne (EOIs closed 25 May 2026).

next_edition_expected: Ozwater'28: 23-25 May 2028, MCEC Melbourne; Ozwater'29: 29-31 May 2029, Adelaide Convention Centre
description: Australia's premier water industry exhibition and conference, run by the Australian Water Association since 1964, bringing together water utilities, engineers and suppliers around a large exhibition floor.
notes: Australia's premier water exhibition and conference, running since 1964 (as Federal Convention). 200+ exhibits. 2026 edition ran 26-28 May at Brisbane Convention & Exhibition Centre; row dated to next edition (Ozwater'27, ICC Sydney).

next_edition_expected: 2028 (calendar realignment announced May 2026; series alternates Melbourne and Sydney; last held 20-23 May 2025 at Sydney Showground)
description: The most comprehensive B2B event for the print, sign and graphics industries in the Asia-Pacific region, running since 1970 and historically co-located with Visual Impact and the Label & Packaging Expo.
notes: The most comprehensive B2B event for the print, sign and graphics industries in ANZ/Asia-Pacific, running since 1970. No 2026/2027 edition - next edition shifted to 2028 in a strategic calendar realignment. Historically co-located with Visual Impact and the Label & Packaging Expo. Organising entity not clearly stated on official site (contact details match Visual Connections) - left blank.

venue: Nyaal Banyul Geelong Convention and Event Centre
description: 18th annual conference & exhibition of the PCO Association - the community gathering for event management professionals across Australia and New Zealand.
target_audience: Professional conference organisers, event managers and event industry suppliers
notes: 500+ delegates expected per Meet Geelong announcement. Dates (6-9 Dec 2026) per Tropical North Queensland event listing; official site confirms Geelong host and registration is open.

organiser: The Pharmacy Guild of Australia (Queensland Branch)
description: The Pharmacy Guild's east coast conference, combining a two-day education programme with a trade exhibition of around 70 stands. The 2025 edition drew 1,800 delegates, giving suppliers direct access to community pharmacy owners and staff. A useful complement to APP for vendors selling into the Australian pharmacy channel.
target_audience: Community pharmacists, pharmacy owners and pharmacy assistants
notes: Attendance and stand figures are from the organiser's published 2025 wrap-up (via Pharmacy Daily). Smaller floor than APP - triaged medium.

description: Australia's peak philanthropy gathering - 1,000+ funders, changemakers and sector leaders. Core conference 8-10 Sep 2026 with fringe masterclasses (7 Sep) and field trips (11 Sep). 2026 theme: Mobilising Generosity.
target_audience: Funders, foundations, trustees, philanthropy advisers and sector leaders
notes: Core dates stored; full program incl. fringe runs 7-11 Sep. Venue within Brisbane not specified on official page.

description: 13th annual forum - Australia's premier event showcasing technologies and innovations driving the future of policing and public safety, from AI and data analytics to crime prevention tools.
target_audience: Senior police leaders, law enforcement technology buyers, public safety agencies
notes: Ran 24-25 Mar 2026. 2027 dates not yet announced.

description: The flagship national gathering of Australia's ports community, run every two years by Ports Australia, bringing together port authorities, terminal operators and maritime infrastructure stakeholders.
notes: Flagship national gathering of Australia's ports community, run every two years by the national member body for ports. 2026 edition in Sydney, 22-24 Sep, with conference dinner at Doltone House; specific conference venue not yet published - left blank. Buyers include port authorities, terminal operators and maritime infrastructure stakeholders.

description: The largest regional mining event in Australia, held for over 30 years at the heart of Central Queensland's coal and resources industry. Hundreds of suppliers, live demonstrations and a free seminar series put vendors directly in front of Bowen Basin operational buyers.
target_audience: Queensland mining operators, engineers, maintenance leads and procurement across the Bowen Basin
notes: Biennial. Exhibitor sources cite 300+ suppliers and 5,000+ visitors in 2024 - not from the official site, so left out of structured fields. Runs 10 days after this research date.

description: Australia's premium retail sourcing event for over 50 years, connecting wholesale brands with the 18,000+ retailers, designers and stylists who visit its two national fairs each year. A pure vendor-meets-buyer trade format for consumer product suppliers entering Australian retail channels.
target_audience: Retail buyers, designers and stylists sourcing wholesale gift, home and lifestyle products
notes: Two editions per year: Sydney (ran 14-17 February 2026 at ICC Sydney) and Melbourne (8-12 August 2026 at MCEC). 18,000+ retailer visitation figure from the official site covers both national events. New 2026 categories: Garden & Outdoor Living (SYD) and Retail Services (MELB).

description: International gambling law and regulation conference convening regulators, industry leaders and researchers. Seventh edition in 2027, including the RTG Global Awards gala on 9 March.
target_audience: Gambling regulators, compliance leaders, legal practitioners, operators and policy stakeholders
notes: Call for speakers open until 11 December 2026 per official site.

description: The anchor show of Food & Hospitality Week, recently rebranded from Foodservice Australia to sharpen its focus on restaurant and institutional foodservice buyers. Combines exhibition aisles with major competitions (Chef of the Year, Pizza Championships) that drive strong operator attendance.
target_audience: Restaurant operators, institutional foodservice procurement (aged care, hospitals, schools, defence), caterers and venue managers
notes: 2026 edition ran 25-27 May at ICC Sydney; 2027 confirmed for 2-4 May at MCEC. Alternates Sydney/Melbourne (next Sydney edition 2028). Aggregators cite ~400 exhibitors/8,000 attendees - noted only.

description: Australia's community-driven retail and ecommerce conference - three high-energy days of operator-led sessions, workshops and an expo floor on the Gold Coast. Its hosted-buyer programme and sponsor packages give vendors curated one-on-one time with high-intent retail decision makers.
target_audience: Retail and ecommerce founders, CEOs and operators from SMB to enterprise brands
notes: Formerly known as Retail Global. 2026 edition ran 5-7 May at Gold Coast Convention Centre; 2027 dates (11-13 May) confirmed on the official site.

description: One of Australia's most established gatherings for senior retail leaders, returning in 2027 for its 16th annual edition. Its sponsorship-driven format concentrates retail CEOs and executive buyers in one room, suiting vendors selling enterprise retail, AI and customer-experience solutions.
target_audience: Retail CEOs, C-suite executives, customer, digital and commercial decision makers
notes: 2026 edition ran 20-21 May at Crown Melbourne. 2027 dates (23-24 March, Melbourne) confirmed on the official site; venue not yet published.

description: A trade-only exhibition for retail technology, store design and operations, expecting 100+ exhibitors and 7,000+ trade visitors across five sectors at MCEC. It targets store-level execution buyers, making it a practical entry point for vendors selling into Australian retail operations.
target_audience: Retailers, brands, retail tech providers, store design and supply chain partners across ANZ and APAC
notes: Attendance (7,000+ trade visitors) and exhibitor count (100+) from the official site. 2026 speakers include Paul Zahra and leaders from Microsoft, Google Cloud and David Jones.

description: Terrapinn's dedicated event for the roads, traffic and construction sector under the theme 'Design. Procure. Deliver.', co-located with MOVE AU at ICC Sydney, connecting suppliers with road agencies and infrastructure delivery teams.
target_audience: Road and traffic authorities, civil contractors, traffic engineers, infrastructure planners and government transport agencies
notes: Co-located with MOVE AU. Free registration for qualified attendees.

next_edition_expected: 2027 (annual; 2026 was the 40th edition)
description: Australia's premier screen industry event, run by Screen Producers Australia, bringing together producers, financiers and buyers for conference sessions and the SPA Connect deal-making market.
notes: Australia's premier screen industry event - one of the largest congregations of screen professionals in the Southern Hemisphere. Includes SPA Connect in-person market (buyers/sellers deal-making) plus a Global Market Online component (21-22 April 2026). 2026 edition has run.

description: Australia's national seafood industry conference (established 1999, FRDC-backed), including the National Seafood Industry Awards and a behind-the-scenes tour of the new Sydney Fish Market.
target_audience: Stakeholders across the seafood supply chain: fishers, aquaculture producers, processors, wholesalers, tech and service providers
notes: Biennial, rotates states. Official site confirms 28-30 July 2026 at ICC Sydney.

description: A one-day pop-up security-technology trade show touring five Australian capital cities each May, run by SEN, connecting installers, integrators and security managers with exhibiting suppliers.
notes: One-day pop-up trade show touring 5 capital cities each May - 2026 tour has run: Brisbane 12 May (RICC), Sydney 14 May (Rosehill Gardens), Melbourne 19 May (MCEC), Adelaide 21 May, Perth 26 May (Crown Perth). Draws 2,000-2,500 attendees across the tour; 21 exhibitors in 2026. Compact format targeting installers, integrators, consultants and security managers. A SecTech NZ tour (Auckland/Wellington/Christchurch) ran June 2026.

description: Australia's most established trade event for the security industry, marking its 40th year in 2026 with a sold-out exhibition floor at ICC Sydney. It spans physical and electronic security with growing converged-cyber content via the ASIAL Security Conference, giving suppliers face time with integrators, consultants and end-user buyers.
target_audience: Security integrators, consultants, facility and operations managers, and enterprise/government security buyers
notes: Primarily physical/electronic security rather than pure cyber - included for its converged security relevance. 40th anniversary edition; floorplan sold out with new space added. Co-located with Fire Security Expo and Integrate 2026.

description: One of Australia's leading SME events, bringing together business owners, founders, industry leaders and government for a free expo and expert-led sessions.
notes: One of Australia's leading SME events, now in its 5th year - brings together business owners, founders, industry leaders and government. Free entry; expo plus expert-led sessions. Dates confirmed by both the official event site and MCEC's venue listing. Organising company (ABF Events per contact details) not confirmed on official site - left blank.

description: Australia's longest-running renewable energy event, reaching its 65th edition in 2027 with 12,000+ delegates and 130+ exhibitors at ICC Sydney. Free registration drives strong installer and developer foot traffic, making it a volume lead-generation platform for smart energy vendors.
target_audience: Solar and storage installers, project leads, developers, policymakers and energy investors
notes: 2026 edition ran 6-7 May at ICC Sydney. The Smart Energy Council also runs one-day state editions (Smart Energy SA, 30 June 2026; Smart Energy Queensland, 8 September 2026 at Royal ICC Brisbane).

next_edition_expected: July 2026 (Royal Randwick, Sydney - exact dates not published on official SMPTE pages at time of research)
organiser: SMPTE Oceania (Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers)
description: The most comprehensive media and broadcast technology event in the Oceania region, run by SMPTE, combining a full broadcast exhibition with a free conference program of keynotes, panels and technical deep-dives across broadcast, streaming, IP infrastructure, cloud workflows and AI.
target_audience: Broadcasters, streaming platforms, media technology engineers, production companies and broadcast equipment vendors
notes: The 2025 edition ran 29-31 July 2025 at Royal Randwick, Sydney. Event listings indicate a July 2026 edition at the same venue, but official 2026 dates were not confirmed on SMPTE's own pages - date field left blank rather than inferred.

description: Australia's premier conference for the self-managed super fund sector, run by the SMSF Association, bringing together advisers, accountants, auditors and trustees around a trade exhibition, delivered in live and virtual formats.
notes: Australia's premier SMSF sector conference - advisers, accountants, auditors and trustees, with trade exhibition. 2026 edition ran 18-20 Feb in Adelaide; National Conference 2027 confirmed for 17-19 Feb 2027 in Melbourne (venue TBA) per SMSF Association events page. Live + virtual formats. Note: the MES live site currently lists this event as 'Gold Coast' - official association source says Melbourne for 2027.

event_name: SPE Asia Pacific Oil & Gas Conference and Exhibition (APOGCE)
description: The Asia Pacific region's premier upstream technical conference and exhibition, returning to Brisbane in 2026. While programme content is technical, the exhibition and sponsorship options connect service and technology vendors with regional operators and engineering buyers.
target_audience: Upstream technical professionals, operators and service companies across the Asia Pacific energy value chain
notes: Industry (not academic) technical conference with a commercial exhibition floor; rotates around Asia Pacific host cities, returning to Australia periodically.

description: One of the Southern Hemisphere's premier sport business summits, backed by the Victorian Government, bringing together sport executives, brands and technology providers for keynotes, a sport-tech exhibition and networking.
notes: One of the Southern Hemisphere's premier sport business summits, backed by the Victorian Government. 2026: 1,000+ delegates from 20+ countries, 75+ speakers, sport tech exhibition, B2B functions; timed ahead of the first NFL regular-season game at the MCG. Tickets from A$1,495.

next_edition_expected: March 2027, Adelaide (confirmed - announced at the close of the 2026 Gold Coast edition; specific venue not yet published; host city rotates annually)
description: The pre-eminent gathering of Australia's general insurance broking industry, hosted by the Steadfast broker network since 2001 with a large sponsor marketplace. The single best access point to Australian insurance brokers for incoming vendors.
target_audience: Insurance brokers in the Steadfast Network, underwriters, insurtechs and service providers
notes: 2026 official site cites 2,000+ attendees and 100+ sponsors; 2027 dates/venue not yet announced - dates left null per verification rules.

description: Australia's largest construction, architecture and infrastructure show, with 16 conference stages, networking parties and a sold-out exhibition floor at ICC Sydney. The highest-volume construction buyer audience in the country for building products and contech vendors.
target_audience: Contractors, developers, architects, civil engineers, housebuilders and government construction buyers
notes: 2026 edition (29-30 April) drew 24,000+ attendees, 708 exhibitors and 560 speakers per the official wrap-up; 2027 dates (28-29 April, 10th anniversary) confirmed officially. Part of the international Build series (London, Chicago).

description: W.Media's convention series bringing Australia's cloud and data centre ecosystem together, with dedicated Data Center, Interconnect, and Cloud/AI/Compute streams across two days at ICC Sydney. Vendors exhibit alongside conference programming to an audience of operators, enterprise IT and infrastructure buyers. Useful, repeatable access for connectivity and infrastructure vendors, with a companion Melbourne edition each autumn.
target_audience: Cloud providers, data centre operators, interconnect providers, enterprise IT and digital infrastructure leaders
notes: Melbourne Cloud & Datacenter Convention ran 23 Apr 2026; the series operates across APAC. Listed on the Data Centres Australia industry body events calendar. Attendance/exhibitor numbers not published - triaged medium.

next_edition_expected: May 2027, Melbourne (expected; annual - the 2026 edition ran 21 May at MCEC with content across three stages)
organiser: RCSA (Recruitment, Consulting and Staffing Association Australia & New Zealand)
description: The peak recruitment industry body's flagship conference for the staffing sector, covering business development, marketing, back-office best practice and leadership. The main channel for rec-tech and services vendors targeting Australian recruitment agencies.
target_audience: Recruitment and staffing agency owners, leaders and consultants across ANZ
notes: One-day, three-stage format; 2027 dates not yet announced - dates left null per verification rules.

description: Tech in Gov is Australia's leading event for government ICT, drawing around 3,000 public-sector attendees to Canberra with 150 speakers across seven conference tracks and a full exhibition floor. Government attends free while suppliers join via a capped Vendor Partner Program, making it the most direct route to Australian public-sector technology buyers for both international entrants and local scale-ups.
target_audience: Federal, state and local government ICT buyers - CIOs, CTOs, CISOs and digital transformation leads
notes: Attendance (3,000) and vendor programme (250 places) published by organiser Terrapinn. Venue confirmed by National Convention Centre Canberra.

description: Australia's premier not-for-profit technology capability-building, upskilling, networking and awards event, covering AI for good, cyber security, digital inclusion, data and ethical technology. Includes the Australian Not-For-Profit Technology Awards.
target_audience: Not-for-profit leaders, IT managers and digital transformation buyers
notes: Ran 6-8 May 2026. Strong vendor channel into NFP tech buyers.

description: The telecom sector's night of nights - a black-tie gala dinner (formerly The ACOMMS) recognising outstanding achievement by operators, technology providers and satellite companies. Sponsorship and table packages put vendors alongside the industry's senior decision-makers in a single room. Strong brand-building and relationship value for companies establishing an Australian telecom presence.
target_audience: Executives from telecom operators, technology providers, satellite companies and the broader Australian telco sector
notes: 2026 is the 20th anniversary edition, from 6:00pm on 15 Jul at the Shangri-La Sydney. Run by the industry peak body (renamed from Communications Alliance); awards managed by TeamSlatts.

description: The AI Summit Australia brings Informa's global AI-for-business event series to Melbourne, co-located with Data Center World Australia at MCEC. Focused on turning AI pilots into commercial outcomes, it gives vendors an exhibition platform in front of enterprise decision-makers, and offers market entrants a fast read on how Australian organisations are actually buying AI.
target_audience: Enterprise leaders and innovators buying commercial AI solutions across Australian industry
notes: Inaugural Australian edition of the global AI Summit series. Co-located with Data Center World Australia (already captured in Run 003) - one combined venue, two event brands.

next_edition_expected: March 2027 (expected; annual - the 14th edition ran 18 March 2026 in Sydney)
organiser: The Australian (News Corp), in partnership with Visy
description: A key fixture on the national corporate calendar, convening the leaders of Australia's $150 billion agribusiness and food sector for a day of high-level strategy, policy and investment discussion. Sponsorship offers vendors brand positioning in front of the industry's most senior buyers.
target_audience: CEOs and senior leaders across Australia's $150bn agribusiness and food sector, plus government and investors
notes: 2026 edition (18 March, Sydney) has passed; 2027 dates not yet announced. Ministerial keynotes are standard (2026: Minister for Agriculture).

description: A B2B business-growth event bringing together entrepreneurs, senior executives and business leaders for exhibitor stands and conference content, part of The Business Show World Series.
notes: B2B business growth event - 8,000+ entrepreneurs, senior executives and business leaders expected; free registration. Part of The Business Show World Series (Miami, Singapore, London editions in 2026).

next_edition_expected: June 2027 (expected; 2026 gala was 18 Jun at ILUMINA, Sydney)
description: Australia's most recognised fintech business awards, celebrating the industry's companies, leaders and innovations each year. The gala night concentrates founders, investors, banks and partners in one room and sells out. Strong brand-building and networking value for both new entrants and scaling locals, though it is not a selling floor.
target_audience: Fintech founders and executives, investors, banks and ecosystem partners
notes: 10th annual edition (2026) sold out; winners announced 18 Jun 2026.

description: The Property Council of Australia's flagship annual congress, gathering the property industry's most senior leaders for three days of programme and networking. Sponsorship is the premium route for proptech and services vendors to reach institutional property buyers.
target_audience: Property developers, investors, fund managers and senior executives across the Australian property industry
notes: 2026 dates (20-22 October) from the official registration page; host city rotates and is not shown on the registration page - venue/city left null per verification rules.

event_name: TheMHS Conference (The Mental Health Services Conference)
description: Australasia's largest and most diverse mental health conference, running for 30+ years and rotating cities annually. 2026 theme: Illuminating Hope, Inclusion & Connection.
target_audience: Mental health clinicians, service managers, policy makers, consumers & carers
notes: Official conference page states dates directly as 4-7 Aug at Hilton Sydney (no ambiguity found on re-check). Attendance ~1,000 delegates per year (third-party sector listing). In-person and online.

event_name: VALA Conference (Libraries, Technology and the Future)
organiser: VALA - Libraries, Technology and the Future Inc.
description: The 23rd VALA conference - Australia's long-running library technology conference for the GLAM sector, traditionally accompanied by a supplier exhibition.
target_audience: Library technology professionals across galleries, libraries, archives & museums (GLAM)
notes: Call for abstracts open as of research date.

event_name: Venture & Capital - Private Market Investment Conference
description: A two-day curated deal-making conference by Wholesale Investor bringing 750+ investors, VCs and family offices together with 50+ vetted companies. Day 1 focuses on innovation; Day 2 covers property and funds.
target_audience: Active investors, VCs, family offices, fund managers and founders seeking capital
ticket_price_range_aud: From $99 early access (2026; pricing subject to change)
notes: Positioned as a deal-making event rather than a trade exhibition; ticketed via Eventbrite.

description: The leading event for the ANZ sign, display, digital/wide-format print and graphics sector, run by Visual Connections Australia, featuring exhibitors and a co-located conference program.
notes: Leading event for the ANZ sign, display, digital/wide-format print and graphics sector - reimagined for 2026 with a co-located conference (25+ speakers, 16 sessions). ~4,000 visitors expected. At Sydney Olympic Park.

description: Western Australia's premier mining event, pairing a two-day conference (opened by state government) with an expansive exhibition floor at PCEC. With WA the powerhouse of Australia's resources sector, it is a strategic entry point for METS vendors targeting the west.
target_audience: WA mining operators, engineers, government and resources supply chain buyers
notes: 2025 edition sold out its exhibition floor per the venue's listing. Tickets for 2026 on sale since February.

description: Australia's largest gathering of waste management and resource recovery professionals, run by RX Australia and co-located with All-Energy Australia and the Greener Infrastructure Conference; free to attend.
notes: Australia's largest gathering of waste management and resource recovery professionals. Free to attend. Co-located with All-Energy Australia and the Greener Infrastructure Conference.

next_edition_expected: 2028 (expected; triennial - 2025 edition ran 21-23 July at Adelaide Convention Centre)
organiser: Expertise Events with the Wine Industry Suppliers Association (WISA)
description: Australia's leading wine industry supplier trade show, held every three years in partnership with the Australian Wine Industry Technical Conference. The premier opportunity for equipment and technology suppliers to reach winery and vineyard buyers nationally.
target_audience: Winemakers, viticulturists, winery engineers and grape and wine sector procurement
notes: One aggregator lists a 21-23 July 2026 edition, but the official site shows the last edition as July 2025 - official source used; date conflict recorded per verification rules.

event_name: World Credit Union Conference / COBA Convention
organiser: World Council of Credit Unions (WOCCU) & Customer Owned Banking Association (COBA)
description: The premier global gathering for credit unions, mutual banks and cooperative financial institutions, co-hosted in 2026 by WOCCU and COBA with an international exhibition floor. Thousands of delegates attend to source technology and service solutions. A concentrated buying audience for fintechs targeting the mutual and customer-owned banking sector in Australia and abroad.
target_audience: Credit union, mutual bank and cooperative financial institution leaders from Australia and worldwide
notes: 2026 edition replaces COBA's annual convention (COBA25 in Brisbane drew 1,000+ delegates); WCUC rotates internationally - first time in Sydney. Future Australian editions expected to revert to the COBA Convention format.
